parsed_review.recommendation: Approve CP00-132 for closeout. The Risk C fixture/evidence/review-readiness catalog is purely additive, synthetic, metadata-only, and read-only; it inherits CP131 verdict-boundary behavior without re-implementing it and enforces every claimed no-write/no-execute boundary.
parsed_review.rationale: Scope and coverage reconcile exactly. The 11 micro-phases (RP02.P08.M06/M07/M08/M09/M10 + RP02.P09.M00-M05) sum to 150 units (3+22+20+20+8+4+8+8+20+20+17), first_unit_id=RP02.P08.M06.S18 and last_unit_id=RP02.P09.M05.S17 match the planned range, and all 150 source_unit_ids are unique (distinct per micro-phase). Hand-verified deliverable counts (implementation 68, test 9, hermes_evidence 38, claude_review 18, security_audit 12, ui 5) and domain counts (fixture_evidence 38, fixture_verdict_validation 18, claude_review_packet 6, review_question 44, fixture_closeout_readiness 14, review_closeout_readiness 30) both total 150 and match the module's own validate() and the external validation script. All 41 distinct behavior kinds are mapped in STATUS_BY_KIND, so no row degrades to the unknown/blocked default. Boundary enforcement is structural, not data-driven: freezeNoWriteResult() applies the no-write flags AFTER spreading row data, so writes_product_state, writes_audit_event, mutates_permission_policy, creates_database_rows, persists_idempotency_keys, acquires/persists locks, executes_rollback/retry/compensation, executes_export_download/external_share, executes_ai_retrieval/analytics_query, grants_human_approval, executes_claude_review, writes_hermes_runtime, implements_ldip, unauthorized_count_exposed, and hidden_field_names_exposed are all hard-coded false and Object.freeze'd and cannot be overridden. Per-domain sub-objects keep receipts reference-only: changed_files_disclose_raw_diff=false, command_result_discloses_raw_output=false, fixture_contains_real_data=false, audit_summary_emitted_to_audit_ledger=false, hidden_template_fields_rendered=false; review_questions keep executes_permission_bypass=false, renders_ui=false, writes_audit_event=false; closeout_readiness keeps commits_pack=false, marks_production_ready=false, command_rerun_executed=false, documentation_published=false; claude_review_packet keeps read_only=true and executes_claude_review=false with exactly_one_valid_pack_review_required=true. CP131 inheritance is read-only function calls whose results are asserted (result_count=10, Claude dependency not executed, PASS_WITH_FINDINGS adjudication, BLOCK prevents production_ready), with no re-implementation. No runtime UI/API route registration is added (runtime_ui_route_added=false, runtime_api_route_added=false; index.js only re-exports). Replay/command anchors are inert strings (replay_persists_state=false, replay_acquires_lock=false). No real client data is materialized; principals/tenants are synthetic; hidden_source_fields are name-only boundary declarations, never values, and never emitted into per-case results. HRX remains embedded (hrx_embedded_boundary), LDIP unimplemented, handoff fixed to CP00-133 / RP02.P09.M05.S18, and the contract schema bump (v0.25->v0.26), current_pack swap, production_ready_flag, public_exports, and no_write_attestation are mutually consistent across contract JSON, module, tests, and the validation script. No P0-P3 issues found.

raw_result.result:
I reviewed the staged CP00-132 diff (contract, new catalog module, README, index re-export, tests, and validation script) against every required boundary. Below is the review verdict as JSON.

No files were modified and no commands were run. The pack is read-only, synthetic, and metadata-only; every required boundary holds, so it does not block closeout.
